title: The Silent Bell
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: An old, stone mountain bell tower overlooks a misty, silent valley. Focus on the hammer.
description: An old, stone mountain bell tower overlooks a misty, silent valley. Focus on the hammer.
observation: The bell-ringer pulls the heavy rope, but the booming tone of the massive bell echoes across the valley early.
reconstruction: Carry the hemp rope, the iron hammer, and the misty valley wind.
attunements: The hemp fibers show sudden, immense friction stress.
The iron head is cold, carrying no residual impact vibration.
The valley echo returns before the bell hammer makes contact.
revelation:
What was wrong: the valley echoed with a toll that the bell had not yet rung.

Why it mattered: the warning of the coming storm was carried by the wind before the metal could strike.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the bell hammer's contact point.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Causality shift located. The copper sound waves disperse before impact.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE ACOUSTIC SWEEP BEGINS.
capture_window.success_text: Timeline isolated: sound wave precedes strike.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE HEMP FIBERS SHOW SUDDEN FRICTION STRESS. The rope stands taut, vibrating with the ghost energy of a pull.
THE IRON HEAD IS COLD. The metal contains no residual warmth or kinetic impact vibration.
THE MIST CARRIES THE SOUND BACK TOWARD THE TOWER. The echo originates from the valley before reaching the bell.
